# browser_oxide — stealth headless browser engine (Rust, Python, MCP)

> A from-scratch headless browser engine in Rust for web scraping, archival, and
> AI agents: real HTML/CSS/DOM parser, V8 JavaScript (via deno_core), own CSS
> engine (Syntax L3, Selectors L4, cascade), arena DOM, Canvas 2D (tiny-skia),
> and its own stealth HTTP/TLS stack (boring2/BoringSSL) with a Chrome/Firefox-
> accurate fingerprint (JA3/JA4, HTTP/2). Not a Chromium fork, not a Firefox
> fork, no CDP driver — the stealth is native to the engine. Renders through
> Cloudflare, Akamai, AWS WAF, and much of DataDome from a from-scratch stack
> (no per-vendor bypass code shipped). MIT OR Apache-2.0, pre-1.0.

- Rust: add via git dependency (`browser = { git = "https://github.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide" }`); crates are `publish = false` pending a single-crate release
- Python: `pip install browser-oxide`
- MCP server (for AI agents): `cargo install --git https://github.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide browser_oxide_mcp` → `browser-oxide-mcp`
- Repository: https://github.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide
- License: MIT OR Apache-2.0 (no GPL/LGPL/AGPL; one MPL-2.0 transitive `cooked-waker`, plus optional `blocker` feature adds MPL-2.0 `adblock` — both `deny.toml` exceptions)
- Workspace: 18 Rust crates (15 engine + host/python/mcp bindings)
- Maturity: pre-1.0, API not yet stable

## Keywords

stealth browser, headless browser, anti-bot, anti-detect browser, web scraping,
Rust browser engine, TLS fingerprint, JA3, JA4, BoringSSL impersonation,
Cloudflare alternative, Akamai, DataDome, PerimeterX, Kasada, Puppeteer
alternative, Playwright alternative, Selenium alternative, undetected browser,
AI agent browser, MCP browser, scraping in Python, scraping in Rust.

## Getting started

- [Getting started — Rust](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/getting-started-rust.md): the `!Send`/LocalSet pattern, `Page::navigate`, content extraction, `challenge_verdict`, `PagePool`
- [Getting started — Python](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/getting-started-python.md): `pip install browser-oxide`; `Browser` / `Page` / `Profile` / `Verdict`
- [Profiles guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/guides/PROFILES.md): identities + multi-profile routing
- [Challenges guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/guides/CHALLENGES.md): verdict semantics + the `ChallengeSolver` extension point
- [Stealth FAQ](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/guides/STEALTH_FAQ.md): what's native vs. not (honest boundary)
- [Benchmark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/BENCHMARK.md): measured anti-bot pass rates
- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): the crates + dependency graph
- [SCOPE.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/browser_oxide/main/SCOPE.md): what it's for / not for

## Top-level Rust API (crate `browser`)

- `browser::Page::navigate(url, profile, max_iterations) -> Result<Page>` — cold, humanized navigation.
- `browser::Page::from_html(html, Some(profile))` — render a literal HTML string (no network).
- `page.title()`, `page.content()` (outerHTML), `page.text_content()`, `page.text_of(selector)` — extract content.
- `page.evaluate(js) -> Result<String>` — run JavaScript in the page realm.
- `page.challenge_verdict() -> ChallengeVerdict` — Pass / ThinShell / RenderIncomplete / EdgeBlock / SensorFail / ChallengeIncomplete (HTTP status lies on challenge pages).
- `browser::PagePool::new(n)` + `pool.navigate(url, profile)` — warm-isolate reuse for benign high-throughput pages.
- `browser::ChallengeSolver` trait + `Page::navigate_with_solvers(...)` — pluggable per-vendor handling (engine ships an empty solver set).
- `protocol::CdpServer::start_navigable(port)` — Puppeteer/Playwright drop-in over WebSocket.

## Python API (`pip install browser-oxide`)

- `Browser(profile=Profile.chrome())` — context-managed; spawns the engine thread.
- `b.navigate(url, max_iterations=5) -> Page` — GIL released during the call.
- `page.url / .title / .html / .text / .verdict / .is_challenge` — properties.
- `page.evaluate(js)`, `page.query_text(selector)` — scripting.
- `Profile.chrome() / .firefox() / .iphone() / .pixel() / .from_file(path)`.
- `Verdict.PASS / THIN_SHELL / RENDER_INCOMPLETE / EDGE_BLOCK / SENSOR_FAIL / CHALLENGE_INCOMPLETE`.

## MCP tools (`browser-oxide-mcp`, stdio JSON-RPC)

- `fetch_page(url, profile?, format?)` — render and return text/html + verdict.
- `evaluate(url, js, profile?)` — render then run JS.
- `check_protection(url, profile?)` — report whether a URL is bot-walled and whether a real render got through.

## Stealth / fingerprint profiles (crate `stealth`)

- `stealth::StealthProfile` — coherent browser identity (UA, screen, locale, GPU, TLS impersonation label, canvas/audio seeds, …).
- `stealth::StealthProfile::load_from_file(path)` — YAML or JSON (auto-detected); runs `validate()`.
- Built-in presets: `chrome_148_{macos,windows,linux,ru,cn,de,jp}`, `firefox_135_{macos,windows,linux}`, `pixel_9_pro_chrome_148`, `iphone_15_pro_safari_18`.
- Example YAML: `crates/stealth/profiles/chrome_148_macos.yaml`.

## Measured coverage (see BENCHMARK.md)

126-site anti-bot corpus, release build, honest gate (rendered tag AND ≥15 KB):
routed best-of-4 ≈ 118/126. Residual that does not pass: Kasada
(canadagoose/hyatt/realtor), DataDome interactive (etsy), plus flaky Akamai
(adidas/homedepot) and a couple of CSR SPAs. Kasada is the open gap — no
open-source tool publicly passes it from scratch.

## Build and test

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace -- --test-threads=1    # V8 isolates are per-thread
```
